hard.next_step: June 15 Rick: Conducted Intake Demo to Alex (info gatherer, reports to Nathan w/findings) Short-term focus: enable intake for easier end-user adoption Longer-term goal: expand into sourcing/contract automation as organizational readiness improves Follow up: Horizon invite, Showpad w/demo-recording.
hard.ais_why: Merlin Intake AI-selection at Shortlisted; customer driving internal AI adoption and change-management planning; Merlin's intake layer is the core of the deal, not an add-on.

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, enthusiastic expansion evaluation against an impossible close date. Alex Becker was sold after the 12 Jun Intake demo ('I like what I see... we chose the right business partner in you because you are always innovating'), but he set the buyer's own adoption horizon as a 'late 27-28 initiative' with a 1-2 year org-wide rollout. The 24 Jul close cannot survive that; the deal is fine, the date is fiction.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 05 May 2026, Shortlisted since 13 May (~54 days in stage), close 24 Jul 2026 (18 days to North Star). Last real buyer touch: 12 Jun Intake demo (Alex Becker), incoming email 05 Jun; rep outreach 15 Jun per Next Step (rep-side, awaiting reply). Forward path (Nathan Hemmer prioritization, commercial validation of the $60k, InfoSec sign-off for the Teams-deployed app, indirect-sourcing user pilot) cannot clear in 18 days, and the buyer explicitly deferred adoption to late 2027-2028. Close date slips 12-18 months to match the buyer's stated timeline.
evidence: Alex Becker (Senior Manager Business Intelligence) bought in after 12 Jun demo: 'I like what I see... we chose the right business partner in you because you are always innovating... I'm looking forward to that journey' (Avoma demo 2026-06-12).

Buyer-stated adoption horizon is late 2027-2028: 'I'm gonna say that is, like, a late 27 28 initiative... we're only a year or two out on that' (Alex Becker, 2026-06-12) — the single reason the 24 Jul date is indefensible.

Nathan Hemmer (Senior Director, Indirect Procurement Global) is the strategic decision-maker: 'Nate is the one... he's gonna set the strategic sail where we wanna go. Right now, I'm just gathering information' (Alex Becker, 2026-06-12); Nathan not engaged since the 07 May review and out until 16 Jun per his 02 Jun email.

This is an expansion on an already-signed Zycus platform contract, not a cold deal: 'we had the same provider, Jagger, for the last 8 years... now we're at this point where contracts are signed. We're all almost ready to go' (Alex Becker, 2026-06-12) — access and trust are inherited.

No commercial or pricing conversation has occurred and the $60k (cut from $110k on 11 May) is unvalidated with the buyer; InfoSec sign-off for the Teams-deployed Merlin Intake app is an unstarted gate ('IT being okay... in regards to information security', Alex Becker, 2026-06-12).

Multi-ERP complexity is a live scoping constraint: '5 different ERP systems, 20 to 30 different business units... we cannot do supplier onboarding' (Alex Becker, 2026-06-12), which caps the touchless orchestration flow.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is qualitative and clearly stated but never quantified. Alex Becker framed the goal as user-adoption and rogue-spend capture: making procurement 'easier for the user through this process' and killing the 'rogue buyer' who 'just gonna pick up the phone' to a supplier off-contract (Avoma demo 2026-06-12). No cost, headcount, cycle-time, or savings figure was put on any call, and Alex himself named the ROI gap: 'individual productivity is increasing, but how does that translate into dollars' (2026-06-12). Quantifying adoption lift, cycle-time reduction, and rogue-spend leakage into a business case is the open move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No one controlled a commercial or pricing conversation on any call, so the EB is not confirmed by a commercial signal, but the evidence points squarely at Nathan Hemmer (Senior Director, Indirect Procurement Global): Alex Becker defers budget and priority to him — 'Nate is the one... he's gonna set the strategic sail where we wanna go' (2026-06-12). Because this is an expansion on an already-signed Zycus platform contract, budget authority sits with the same indirect-procurement leadership Zycus already sold — access is inherited, engagement is the gap (Nathan out until 16 Jun per his 02 Jun email, not on the 12 Jun demo). The move is to convert Alex's findings into a direct prioritization conversation with Nathan, not to re-map an unknown EB.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria surfaced through the 12 Jun use-case walkthrough are adoption-first: an 'interface-agnostic', no-training chat experience ('the end user should not have to care about the system... I have a chat interface, I wanna buy a mouse'), catalog and past-requisition reuse, and punch-out / L2 external-catalog search across Dell, CDW, and Amazon Business (Alex Becker, 2026-06-12). Alex confirmed fit on the core intake flow ('That is beautiful. I don't even have to know where to go... it just does it all for me') but flagged a hard constraint: with 5 ERPs and no supplier-onboarding module in scope, the full touchless orchestration flow cannot be realized, so criteria must be scoped to catalog/requisition/punch-out. These are confirmed from the call, not inferred.

decision_process.narrative: Reading the call sequence: 07 May was a joint Intake+Merlin review; 12 Jun was a deeper Intake demo where Alex Becker attended as the designated information-gatherer with new owner Rick Taranek (after Kevin Cipollaro's 29 May handoff). The process Alex described is: he gathers findings, reports to Nathan Hemmer who 'sets the strategic sail', and the organization then decides on readiness — explicitly a late 2027-2028 timeline. The approval chain above Nathan (CPO / IT for InfoSec) is not yet mapped, and no formal evaluation gate, pricing round, or contracting step has been initiated for this intake scope. The next process step the buyer owns is Nathan's prioritization decision.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have opened for this Merlin Intake scope: no MSA/Order Form/SOW discussion, no legal, no procurement channel, and InfoSec for the Teams-deployed app is an unstarted gate that Alex flagged as a real hurdle ('IT being okay... in regards to information security', 2026-06-12). The mitigant is that the base platform contract is already signed and in implementation ('contracts are signed, we're all almost ready to go', 2026-06-12), so an expansion Order Form can ride the existing MSA rather than start cold. Visibility would come from confirming with Alex/Nathan whether intake is added to the signed agreement as an add-on Order Form.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is concrete and owner-attributed. Alex Becker owns two problems: legacy-vendor stagnation ('we had the same provider, Jagger, for the last 8 years... the owners wanna sell, so they're not really motivated. The industry is changing. They don't care', 2026-06-12) and low user adoption of procurement tooling driven by a training/UX burden ('we're trying to avoid all this specific training of learning the tool'). He also named rogue/off-contract buying and the 'last mile AI problem' of turning tool usage into measurable value. Confirmed pain; the inferred extension is that multi-ERP fragmentation (5 ERPs, 20-30 BUs) compounds the adoption problem.

champion.narrative: Alex Becker is a developing, enthusiastic champion but an unproven one. What has developed: he is genuinely advocating ('we chose the right business partner in you because you are always innovating'), he is actively pulling engagement (asked for AI-console/roadmap material, Horizon event details, and an L2-punch-out feasibility check), and he owns the internal narrative to Nathan. What is still missing: he self-described as an information-gatherer and business partner, not a decision-maker or budget owner ('Right now, I'm just gathering information'), his influence with Nathan is untested, and no user community or second thread is engaged behind him. Securing him means arming him with a scoped pilot business case he can carry to Nathan.

competition.narrative: There is no active vendor bake-off for Merlin Intake — Zycus is effectively sole-source on an account it already won at the platform layer, with Jaggaer (Jagger) the displaced 8-year incumbent that lost on product stagnation ('the owners wanna sell, so they're not really motivated', Alex Becker 2026-06-12). The real competitor is do-nothing / delay: the buyer's own late-2027-2028 adoption horizon, legacy-user resistance ('I don't need no chat interface, I can fill this form out within 15 seconds'), multi-ERP complexity, and InfoSec caution. We win not by beating a rival but by shrinking the timeline with a scoped Q4 pilot that proves adoption lift and rides the signed platform momentum.

deal_movement.summary: Live expansion in discovery/validation on an already-won account. Last real buyer touch was the 12 Jun Intake demo (Alex Becker, strongly positive) plus an incoming email 05 Jun; rep outreach logged 15 Jun (rep-side, awaiting buyer reply). Scope was halved on 11 May and ownership transferred to Rick Taranek on 29 May.

items.change: Amount cut $110,000 -> $60,000 (scope narrowed to iSaaS + Merlin Intake)
Owner transferred Kevin Cipollaro -> Rick Taranek (rep handoff)
Intake Management demo delivered to Alex Becker; strong positive reception

competitive_position.summary: No live vendor fight: Zycus is sole-source for Merlin Intake on an account where the base platform contract is already signed, and Jaggaer (the 8-year incumbent) is being displaced, lost on product stagnation. The single strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing / delay — the buyer's own stated 'late 27-28' adoption horizon (Alex Becker, 12 Jun), legacy-user resistance, multi-ERP complexity, and InfoSec caution. We win by carving a small Q4 2026 / Q1 2027 pilot with indirect-sourcing power users that quantifies adoption and ROI, framing intake as Phase 1a of the signed conversion, and using Horizon (21-23 Sep) for peer/exec pull.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / delay (organizational readiness)
competitors.quote: I'm gonna say that is, like, a late 27 28 initiative... we have to see when the crowd would be ready for a change like that... it'll be a little while before we get users to create a requisition in the chat, but I think we're only a year or two out.
competitors.how_we_win: Carve a small Q4-2026 pilot with indirect-sourcing power users that proves adoption lift and ROI, frame intake as Phase 1a of the signed platform, and pull Alex + Nathan into Horizon (21-23 Sep) for peer validation.

competitors.name: Jaggaer (Jagger) — displaced incumbent
competitors.quote: we had the same provider, Jagger, for the last 8 years... the owners wanna sell, so they're not really motivated. The industry is changing. They don't care.
competitors.how_we_win: Already lost the platform decision (Zycus contract signed and in implementation); reinforce Zycus's single-source-code, 50%-in-R&D innovation cadence vs Jaggaer stagnation. Legacy-user familiarity with Jaggaer forms feeds do-nothing resistance, but Jaggaer is not competing for intake.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on the core adoption-first intake flow; a real constraint on full touchless orchestration due to multi-ERP setup and no supplier-onboarding module in scope.

items.criterion: Interface-agnostic, no-training chat intake for end users
items.quote: That is beautiful. I don't even have to know where to go... it just does it all for me. So my opinion, this is interface agnostic.

items.criterion: Catalog + past-requisition reuse with current pricing
items.quote: somebody bought the exact same thing from the supplier in the past, so I can just recreate the order. I just need to get the new price.

items.criterion: Full multi-request orchestration (sourcing -> contract -> onboarding -> PR)
items.quote: I have 5 different ERP system, 20 to 30 different business units... we cannot do supplier onboarding. So suppliers have to exist in the ERP system to flow from the requisition to the purchase order.

items.criterion: External-catalog / L2 punch-out search (Dell, CDW, Amazon Business)
items.quote: It'll go through those 3 catalogs, but look at past requisitions of our buyers and say, hey, here's the mouse that you could be buying.

items.requirement: Send AI-console / product roadmap material on the release cadence.
items.quote: send me a little bit more information about the iConsole where this is... just seeing this road map would be very, very interesting to me on a regular cadence.

items.requirement: Resend the Horizon event (Beaver Creek, 21-23 Sep) invite and details.
items.quote: I just, like, looked in my Outlook. I couldn't find it. But, yeah, if you can send me that again, I'll take a look.

items.deliverable: Confirm to Alex whether L2 external-catalog (Amazon Business punch-out) search results surface inside Merlin Intake chat.
items.grounding_quote: What I can do, I can also check internally if the l two search results are also available in this chart, and I can confirm you back.

items.deliverable: Share the demo-recording link (Showpad) and the meeting-recording folder Rick offered.
items.grounding_quote: there's a link, and in there, I'll also give you another link that takes you to this meeting's recording.

items.deliverable: Name the indirect-sourcing commodity team lead(s) to bring into a pilot / Horizon.
items.grounding_quote: we need an indirect source sourcing commodity team lead in there, right, that can is on the floor every day doing these kind of things. It might be very beneficial for them to attend.

items.area: Commercial / pricing never validated with buyer
items.quote: No pricing, term, or commercial conversation on any call; $60k logged (cut from $110k on 11 May) unconfirmed with the buyer.

items.area: InfoSec / IT sign-off for Teams-deployed Merlin Intake app
items.quote: IT being okay with what is happening here in regards to information security.

items.area: User community (indirect-sourcing leads) not yet engaged
items.quote: we need an indirect source sourcing commodity team lead in there... It might be very beneficial for them to attend.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a healthy expansion stuck behind the buyer's own timeline and a single info-gatherer thread.
flags: Multi-thread past Alex: get Nathan Hemmer into a direct prioritization conversation and name the indirect-sourcing commodity leads whose adoption decides it.
Beat do-nothing by scoping a small Q4-2026 intake pilot with adoption and ROI metrics, framed as Phase 1a of the signed platform, not a new 2027-28 initiative.
Reset the forecast and close date to the buyer's stated horizon; the $60k Best Case / 24 Jul date is indefensible and hides a real, live deal.

items.name: Nathan Hemmer
items.title: Senior Director, Indirect Procurement Global
items.sentiment: Strategic owner who sets priority and budget for indirect procurement; not yet engaged on intake. Alex: 'Nate is the one... he's gonna set the strategic sail where we wanna go.' Out until 16 Jun per his 02 Jun email; inferred EB, access inherited from the signed platform relationship.
items.risk: Not engaged on this scope; the deal cannot advance to commercial without his prioritization. Engagement, not access, is the gap.
items.source: Avoma review 2026-05-07; Task/email 2026-06-02 (Nathan Hemmer); Avoma demo 2026-06-12 (Alex Becker)

items.name: Alex Becker
items.title: Senior Manager Business Intelligence
items.sentiment: Enthusiastic, developing champion and designated information-gatherer. 'I like what I see... we chose the right business partner in you because you are always innovating... I'm looking forward to that journey.' Actively pulling roadmap, Horizon, and L2-punch-out follow-ups.
items.risk: Info-gatherer and business partner, not decision-maker or budget owner; influence with Nathan untested and no second thread behind him.

items.name: Indirect-sourcing commodity team leads
items.title: Not yet named (day-to-day procurement users)
items.sentiment: Unknown; their adoption is the critical success factor and no user voice is in the evaluation yet.
items.risk: Unengaged user community; pilot design and adoption proof require them. Single-threaded to Alex without user validation.

champion_strength.summary: Alex Becker is an enthusiastic, developing champion: actively advocating and pulling engagement (roadmap, Horizon, L2-punch-out check) after the 12 Jun demo, but self-described as an information-gatherer, not the decision-maker or budget owner. What has developed is genuine advocacy and momentum; what is missing is proven influence with Nathan Hemmer and any second/user thread. Arm him with a scoped pilot business case to carry upward.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake is the core of the deal, not an add-on; the AI intake/agentic story landed well with Alex, who explicitly wants the interface-agnostic, no-training vision. Positioning is strong; the constraint is buyer readiness, not our narrative.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7.0 / Moderate is corroborated by the calls: genuine AI appetite ('I'll take advantage of anything AI can give me today') but adoption deferred to late 2027-2028 by the 'last mile AI problem' and legacy-user resistance. Excited but not yet ready to act.

items.detail: Buyer's own adoption horizon is 'late 27-28' with a 1-2 year org rollout; the 24 Jul close is impossible.
Legacy-user resistance to a chat interface ('I can fill this form out within 15 seconds') and cultural AI-adoption drag across the org.
Scope halved $110k -> $60k on 11 May; no commercial conversation has validated the number with the buyer.
5 ERPs / 20-30 business units and no supplier-onboarding module in scope cap the full touchless orchestration flow.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer is engaged and positive but pacing the deal to a distant horizon; momentum is rep-led, not buyer-pulled. Not cooling, but not accelerating.
items: Strong verbal buy-in on 12 Jun demo (Alex Becker).
Buyer deferred timeline to late 2027-2028 and did not request a next meeting.
Engagement single-threaded to an information-gatherer; decision-maker Nathan not yet engaged on intake.

items.action: Co-author a scoped Q4 intake pilot and one-page business case with Alex Becker.
items.trigger: Alex bought in but pacing to late 27-28; needs an internal artifact to carry to Nathan.
items.expected_effect: Converts enthusiasm into a fundable near-term pilot and shrinks the do-nothing timeline.

items.action: Book a prioritization call with Nathan Hemmer to sponsor the intake pilot.
items.trigger: Alex reports findings to Nathan who 'sets the strategic sail'; Nathan back from leave after 16 Jun.
items.expected_effect: Engages the economic buyer and unlocks the path from exploration to commercial.

items.action: Register Alex and a named indirect-sourcing lead for Horizon (21-23 Sep) and send roadmap material.
items.trigger: Alex asked for Horizon details and roadmap cadence and named the need for a commodity team lead.
items.expected_effect: Creates peer/exec pull and a second thread into the user community that decides adoption.

scope_change.detail: Amount halved on 2026-05-11 as the buyer scoped this to a phase-one intake conversion and explicitly deferred sourcing/contract automation to a 'late 27-28' organizational-readiness horizon (Alex Becker, 12 Jun).

expansion_context.prior_opp: Fortive Phase-1 Zycus eProcurement (iSaaS) conversion — signed and in implementation
expansion_context.note: Access inherited from the prior win: 'we're trying to just do a conversion from what we have today to your system... contracts are signed. We're all almost ready to go' (Alex Becker, 12 Jun). Do not flag no-exec-access; budget authority sits with the same indirect-procurement leadership already sold. The gap is expansion-specific prioritization, not access.

customer_preference.evidence: Alex Becker: 'I like what I see... we chose the right business partner in you because you are always innovating' and 'That is beautiful... this is interface agnostic' (2026-06-12); Zycus-specific praise, no competitor lean.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (adoption, rogue-spend, legacy-vendor stagnation); ROI explicitly unquantified — 'how does that translate into dollars' (Alex Becker, 2026-06-12).

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities last 120d; field history to 2026-05-05 creation
gaps: No economic-buyer commercial signal on any call; EB (Nathan Hemmer) inferred from deference, not confirmed by a pricing conversation.
No InfoSec/IT stakeholder identified for the Teams-app deployment gate.
Indirect-sourcing user community not yet named or engaged.
